About: It is about a Korean state that unified and ruled the Korean Peninsula.
Affects: It affects the modern-day Korean identity.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Later Three Kingdoms period.
It is associated with the establishment of Joseon.
It is associated with the ruling class of the northern kingdom of Balhae.
It is associated with the earlier Three Kingdoms of Korea.
It is associated with the Golden Age of Buddhism in Korea.
It is associated with the Middle East.
It is associated with the Yuan dynasty.
It is associated with the Red Turban Rebels from China.
It is associated with professional Japanese pirates.
It is associated with the Ming dynasty.
FoundedBy: It was founded by general Wang Kŏn.
HasA: It has 70 temples in the capital alone in the 11th century.
It has a successor state to Later Goguryeo and Goguryeo.
It has a prominent maritime hojok based in Kaesong.
It has a strong familial kinship with Balhae.
It has a "relative country" and "married country" relationship with Balhae.
It has a "true national unification" by Korean historians.
It has a Northern Expansion Doctrine to reclaim ancestral lands.
It has a coup d'état led by General Yi Sŏnggye.
It has a national policy of integrating Baekje and Goguryeo refugees.
It has a new central army called the Ku-sŏdang.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by what has been called a "true national unification" by Korean historians.
It is characterized by the individual identities of Goguryeo, Baekje and Silla being successfully merged into a single entity.
It is characterized by the name "Korea" being derived from its name.
It is characterized by being a successor state to Later Goguryeo and Goguryeo.
It is characterized by being the "Golden Age of Buddhism" in Korea.
It is characterized by Buddhism achieving its highest level of influence in Korean history.
It is characterized by commerce flourishing.
It is characterized by merchants coming from as far as the Middle East.
It is characterized by its capital being a center of trade and industry.
It is characterized by great achievements in Korean art and culture.
Influenced: It influenced the modern-day Korean identity.
It influenced the establishment of Joseon.
It influenced the ruling class of the northern kingdom of Balhae.
It influenced the earlier Three Kingdoms of Korea.
It influenced the Golden Age of Buddhism in Korea.
It influenced the Middle East.
It influenced the Yuan dynasty.
It influenced the Red Turban Rebels from China.
It influenced professional Japanese pirates.
It influenced the Ming dynasty.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the erratic Taebong ruler Kung Ye.
It was influenced by the Later Three Kingdoms period.
It was influenced by the ruling class of the northern kingdom of Balhae.
It was influenced by the earlier Three Kingdoms of Korea.
It was influenced by the Golden Age of Buddhism in Korea.
It was influenced by the Middle East.
It was influenced by the Yuan dynasty.
It was influenced by the Red Turban Rebels from China.
It was influenced by professional Japanese pirates.
It was influenced by the Ming dynasty.
IsA: It is a successor state to Later Goguryeo and Goguryeo.
It is a vassal state of the Yuan dynasty.
KnownFor: It is known for its "true national unification".
It is known for incorporating much of the ruling class of the northern kingdom of Balhae.
It is known for the merging of individual identities into a single entity.
It is known for being the "Golden Age of Buddhism" in Korea.
It is known for Buddhism achieving its highest level of influence in Korean history.
It is known for flourishing commerce.
It is known for merchants coming from as far as the Middle East.
It is known for great achievements in Korean art and culture.
It is known for constantly wrestling with northern empires such as the Liao and Jin.
It is known for its Northern Expansion Doctrine.
